name: ChatGPT Memory Sources + Excel/Sheets Integration
launch_signal: May 5, 2026 ChatGPT release — new default model (GPT-5.5 Instant) + Memory Sources transparency + Excel/Sheets integration GA
core_positioning: ChatGPT becomes a persistent, personalized AI assistant that shows its reasoning sources and works natively inside productivity apps
problem_it_solves: Users repeat context every session; opaque AI memory; AI assistance limited to the chat window instead of where work happens
user_visibility: High — affects all ChatGPT users as new default model and visible Memory Sources UI in every personalized response
access_barrier_change: Excel/Google Sheets integration GA globally (previously limited). Memory Sources available across all consumer plans.
workflow_change: Users can now see which memories/files/Gmail shaped their ChatGPT response and edit outdated context. Spreadsheet work happens inside the tool, not copy-paste between apps.
distribution_change: Excel and Google Sheets sidebar integration puts ChatGPT inside the world's most-used productivity apps
new_product_form: Memory-auditable AI assistant + embedded spreadsheet AI agent
interaction_pattern: Tap 'sources' button on personalized responses to audit and edit what the model remembered. Natural language in sidebar to build/fix spreadsheets.
user_scenarios: Content creator asks ChatGPT to write in their style — checks which past conversations it used, removes outdated preferences
Marketer builds a budget tracker directly in Google Sheets using natural language ChatGPT sidebar
Designer uses ChatGPT to understand/fix a complex Excel formula without leaving the spreadsheet
business_model: Free (basic) + Plus/Pro/Business/Enterprise subscriptions; Excel/Sheets usage limits per plan tier
risk_caveat: Memory Sources described as 'not full auditability yet' — users may over-trust AI memory transparency. Gmail integration raises privacy concerns.

good: Memory Sources transparency seen as trust-building feature
Spreadsheet integration GA praised for productivity workflow unification
bad: Memory transparency is partial — not all factors shown
Gmail integration opt-in but privacy-sensitive

market_signal_strength: Very high — affects 200M+ ChatGPT users on May 5 (within collection window)
relevance_to_us: High — Memory Sources pattern is directly applicable to skill/workflow product design. Transparency of AI reasoning/context is a key UX challenge we face.
one_line_judgment: ChatGPT adds auditable memory sourcing and global spreadsheet integration on May 5 — a meaningful step toward persistent, transparent AI assistance at mass scale.

name: Adobe Firefly AI Assistant
launch_signal: Public beta April 27, 2026; announced April 15. Adobe for Creativity connector in Claude launched April 28.
core_positioning: AI creative agent that orchestrates multi-step workflows across 60+ Creative Cloud tools from a conversational interface
problem_it_solves: Multi-step creative workflows require switching between apps (Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Illustrator) manually; AI was confined to single-tool assistants
user_visibility: High — public beta rolling out to Creative Cloud subscribers. Adobe for Creativity connector brings tools into Claude interface.
access_barrier_change: Public beta (previously private preview). Creative Cloud subscription required. Adobe for Creativity connector available in Claude (no separate Adobe login needed).
workflow_change: Describe outcome in natural language → assistant executes multi-app workflow → user reviews and refines. Pre-built skills for batch editing, mood boards, portrait retouching, social variations, product mockups.
distribution_change: Adobe tools now accessible inside Claude via connector (Apr 28) — extends reach beyond Adobe subscribers to Claude users
new_product_form: Creative agent that spans multiple professional creative apps (not a single-app assistant)
interaction_pattern: Conversational interface with context-aware controls; user stays in control throughout; outputs remain editable in native Adobe formats
user_scenarios: Social media manager describes 'generate 5 Instagram variations of this product photo' — agent uses Photoshop + Firefly across batch
Video editor asks for 'add music and color grade this footage' — agent uses Premiere + Lumetri + Firefly Audio
Claude user retouches photo using Adobe for Creativity connector without switching to Photoshop
business_model: Creative Cloud subscription (All Apps plan) — no separate AI fee announced for beta
risk_caveat: Public beta may have quality/reliability gaps. 60+ tools = complex orchestration surface area prone to errors. Borderline window (Apr 27/28 vs Apr 29 window start).

good: TechCrunch and 9to5Mac coverage emphasize multi-app orchestration as significant advance
Anthropic partnership (Claude connector) signals enterprise AI legitimacy
bad: Beta status means production reliability unknown
Creative Cloud subscription cost ($60/mo All Apps) limits accessibility

market_signal_strength: Very high — Adobe has 33M Creative Cloud subscribers; multi-app creative agent is category-defining
relevance_to_us: Very high — directly competitive and informative for our AI-native creative tool positioning. Multi-step creative workflow agent with skill-based pre-built actions is the exact product paradigm we are building toward.
one_line_judgment: Adobe's Firefly AI Assistant orchestrates multi-step workflows across 60+ Creative Cloud apps from natural language — the first serious enterprise creative agent at scale.

name: Mistral Vibe Remote Agents + Le Chat Work Mode
launch_signal: May 2, 2026 — Mistral Medium 3.5 public preview + remote cloud agents in Vibe + Le Chat Work Mode all launched together
core_positioning: Cloud-based async coding agents that run while users walk away + enterprise work orchestration in Le Chat
problem_it_solves: Local coding agents require keeping laptop open and block the machine; enterprise workers need multi-tool orchestration (GitHub, Linear, Slack, Teams) in one interface
user_visibility: Medium — Vibe CLI users + Le Chat Pro subscribers; open weights allow self-deployment
access_barrier_change: Remote agents open cloud execution beyond local CLI. Le Chat Work Mode available to Pro subscribers. Open-weight model enables self-hosted deployments.
workflow_change: Teleport local coding session to cloud → close laptop → get notification when agent finished with draft PR. Le Chat Work Mode: describe complex multi-step task → parallel tool calls → consolidated output.
distribution_change: Integration with GitHub, Linear, Jira, Sentry, Slack, Teams extends Vibe agents into enterprise workflows
new_product_form: Async cloud coding agent with state preservation + enterprise work orchestration chat
interaction_pattern: CLI command to teleport session to cloud; notifications on completion; Le Chat: natural language request with tool integrations auto-routed
user_scenarios: Developer pushes complex refactor task to Vibe cloud agent before a meeting — returns to find draft PR ready
PM asks Le Chat Work Mode to research competitive landscape, summarize in Notion, and create Linear tickets — agent executes all in parallel
Small team self-hosts Mistral Medium 3.5 on 4 GPUs for private coding agent
business_model: Le Chat Pro subscription + Vibe CLI enterprise plan + Mistral API ($1.50/$7.50 per M tokens)
risk_caveat: Cloud agent sandbox isolation needs security validation for enterprise use. Work Mode quality dependent on Mistral Medium 3.5 reasoning limits.

good: Async cloud execution pattern widely seen as the right UX for long-running coding tasks
Open-weight backing means teams can verify/audit agent behavior
bad: Vibe still less known than Cursor/Windsurf in developer community
Enterprise integrations (Jira, Linear) need proven reliability

market_signal_strength: Medium-high — async cloud coding agent is a meaningful product pattern, but Mistral's consumer reach is smaller than OpenAI/Anthropic
relevance_to_us: Very high — async cloud coding agent pattern directly applicable to our skill/workflow system design. Self-hosted option + enterprise integrations are key workflow infrastructure signals.
one_line_judgment: Mistral launches async cloud coding agents on May 2 — developers teleport sessions to the cloud, walk away, and get draft PRs back powered by an open-weight model.

name: Cursor 3.x — Agents Window, Fleet Management, Team Marketplace
launch_signal: Multiple releases within window: May 1 (Team Marketplace), May 4 (Enterprise model controls + usage analytics). Cursor 3 core (Apr 2) provides context.
core_positioning: AI IDE evolving into agent fleet management — developers run and manage multiple parallel AI agents instead of writing code directly
problem_it_solves: Developers limited to one agent at a time, requiring active supervision; admins lack control over model access and spend
user_visibility: High — Cursor has large developer mindshare. Team Marketplace and Enterprise controls affect team leads and admins.
access_barrier_change: Team Marketplace (May 1): admins can now create team plugin bundles without connecting a repo first. Enterprise model access controls (May 4) give granular allow/blocklists.
workflow_change: Agents Window: run multiple parallel agents (local, worktree, cloud, SSH) in tiled layout. Seamless handoff between local and cloud. Launch agents from mobile/web/Slack/GitHub.
distribution_change: Agent launch from Slack, GitHub, Linear (mobile + external) extends Cursor beyond the IDE window
new_product_form: Agent fleet manager with marketplace for bundled skills/rules/MCPs + enterprise governance layer
interaction_pattern: Tiled agent sessions in IDE; Agents Window sidebar for monitoring parallel runs; admins set model policies; Team Marketplace distributes curated skill bundles
user_scenarios: Developer runs 3 parallel agents: one fixing tests, one writing docs, one implementing a feature — monitors all in tiled view
Engineering manager sets team-wide model blocklist and soft spend limits with 50%/80%/100% alerts
Team lead creates a Marketplace package bundling company-specific MCP servers, rules, and skills — marks as Required for all team members
business_model: Pro ($20/mo) + Business/Enterprise tiers with admin controls; Cloud Agents billed per compute
risk_caveat: Agent fleet quality depends on underlying model quality; parallel execution adds complexity and cost. Enterprise controls are new (May 4) — reliability track record limited.

good: Agent fleet paradigm widely seen as the right direction for developer productivity
Tiled layout and Team Marketplace praised for team coordination
bad: Increased complexity of managing multiple agents simultaneously
Cloud agent costs can accumulate quickly without careful spend controls

market_signal_strength: High — Cursor is market-leading AI IDE with strong developer mindshare. Fleet management + marketplace signal a platform play.
relevance_to_us: Very high — Cursor's Team Marketplace (bundled skills/MCP/rules) is directly analogous to our skill system product vision. Fleet management pattern is directly applicable to our agent/workflow infrastructure.
one_line_judgment: Cursor ships a Team Marketplace for bundled skills and enterprise model governance on May 1-4 — signaling the AI IDE is becoming a platform for skill/agent distribution.

name: Perplexity Health + Premium Health Sources
launch_signal: May 1 (Health platform GA), May 5 (Premium Health Sources with NEJM/BMJ integration). Personal Computer for Mac (Apr 16) adds context.
core_positioning: Perplexity Health unifies personal health data from wearables/EHRs/labs with clinician-grade medical knowledge for AI-assisted health management
problem_it_solves: Health data is fragmented across devices and apps; AI health answers lack peer-reviewed clinical backing
user_visibility: Medium — Pro/Max subscribers in US (May rollout). Premium Health Sources adds NEJM, BMJ integration.
access_barrier_change: Pro/Max subscription required. iOS and web. Oura and Function Health integrations coming soon.
workflow_change: Apple Health, Fitbit, Ultrahuman, Withings, Clue data unified into AI health assistant. Premium sources add clinical-grade answers.
distribution_change: Personal Computer for Mac (Apr 16) enables 24/7 background AI across local apps. Computer for Enterprise ($325/seat/mo) targets enterprise.
new_product_form: Unified personal health + clinician-grade knowledge AI (not just a search engine)
interaction_pattern: Conversational health queries backed by personal wearable data + peer-reviewed medical journals
user_scenarios: User asks about sleep trend from Oura ring data — health AI answers with wearable data + clinical context
Designer managing chronic condition gets AI-assisted medication question answered with NEJM-sourced context
risk_caveat: Health AI carries liability risk; medical advice disclaimers essential. US-only rollout limits reach. Excluded from our main focus (health vertical) unless it informs creative/productivity patterns.

good: Premium health sources (NEJM, BMJ) seen as meaningful trust signal
Wearable data integration praised for personalized health context
bad: US-only limits global relevance
Health AI liability concerns persist

market_signal_strength: Medium — health vertical is strong signal but outside our core creative/productivity focus
relevance_to_us: Low-medium — health vertical is excluded per scope. However, Perplexity Computer (Personal + Enterprise) pattern of 24/7 background OS-level AI agent is highly relevant for creative workflow tools.
one_line_judgment: Perplexity Health launches with NEJM/BMJ integration on May 5 — clinician-grade AI health assistant, but the more relevant signal is Personal Computer's 24/7 background agent model for workflow tools.

name: Airbyte Agents — Context Store + MCP
launch_signal: May 5, 2026 launch — Airbyte Agents with Context Store addresses #1 root cause of production agent failure (data layer)
core_positioning: Pre-indexed unified data layer for AI agents that eliminates multi-API call chains and reduces token consumption
problem_it_solves: Most AI agent production failures stem from data failures (fragmented APIs, missing context) rather than model failures
user_visibility: Medium — developer/infrastructure tool. MCP compatibility means Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor users can access without direct Airbyte integration.
access_barrier_change: Available via MCP (low integration barrier for Claude/ChatGPT/Cursor users) + native SDK for custom agent teams
workflow_change: Pre-index Salesforce, Zendesk, Jira, Slack data into Context Store → agents query one endpoint instead of chaining 5-6 API calls → dramatically lower latency and token cost
distribution_change: MCP compatibility puts Airbyte's data layer inside Claude, ChatGPT, and Cursor without new integrations
new_product_form: AI agent data infrastructure layer (Context Store) positioned as the 'plumbing that makes agents reliable'
interaction_pattern: Agents query Context Store via MCP or SDK; Context Store handles freshness, search optimization, and multi-source consolidation transparently
user_scenarios: Agent team pulls customer context from Salesforce, Zendesk, Jira — 1 Context Store call instead of 3 separate API chains
Creative tool agent queries project history, asset library, team notes — unified via Context Store
business_model: Enterprise SaaS + open-source Airbyte core (data connectors). Context Store is a new premium feature.
risk_caveat: Infrastructure layer — success depends on connector coverage and data freshness guarantees. Enterprise data compliance (SOC2, GDPR) needs validation.

good: Addresses a real problem (data layer as root cause of agent failures) with strong resonance in developer community
bad: New product — no production track record yet

market_signal_strength: Medium — infrastructure signal; strong for teams building production agents but limited consumer visibility
relevance_to_us: High — directly relevant for our agent/workflow infrastructure. Context Store pattern (pre-indexed, unified data layer) is a key design consideration for our skill system's data access layer.
one_line_judgment: Airbyte launches a unified pre-indexed Context Store on May 5 — collapses 5-6 agent API calls into one and integrates via MCP with Claude, ChatGPT, and Cursor.

name: Midjourney V8.1 Alpha — Image + Video Expansion
launch_signal: V8 Alpha: March 2026. V8.1 Alpha: April 14, 2026. Video V1 in web beta. Video V2 in development.
core_positioning: Midjourney transitions from image-only to an image+video creative platform with 5x faster generation and 2K native resolution
problem_it_solves: Creators need video generation alongside image generation without switching tools; current AI video tools are expensive or slow
user_visibility: Medium — V8 at alpha.midjourney.com (separate from production); V7 still default on main site
access_barrier_change: Alpha access at alpha.midjourney.com. Video V1 in web beta. Pricing: ~25x cheaper than commercial AI video tools (1 credit per second of video = roughly the same as an image).
workflow_change: Animate button in 2026 Web Editor converts images to 21-second video clips. V8 brings faster iteration (5x) for image exploration.
distribution_change: Web Editor with integrated animate button centralizes image+video creation in one interface. Video V2 planned to extend clip lengths further.
new_product_form: Integrated image + video creative tool (vs separate tools like Runway/Kling)
interaction_pattern: Image generation with V8 (5x faster, 2K native, better text) → Animate button → 21s video. Moodboard/style reference workflow preserved.
user_scenarios: Graphic designer creates product mockup in V8, animates it into a 21-second social video at 25x lower cost than Runway
Creative director uses V8's better text rendering for typographic design generation
Brand team generates image + animated version in one tool without Runway subscription
business_model: Subscription-based credit system. Standard plans: $10-120/mo. Video ~1 credit/second (same as images).
risk_caveat: V8 still alpha (not production). V8.1 alpha released Apr 14 — outside the collection window start (Apr 29). Video V2 not yet released. V7 remains production default.

good: 5x speed improvement and 2K native resolution widely praised
Video pricing (~25x cheaper than competitors) seen as major differentiator
bad: Alpha status means V8 quality inconsistent vs V7 production
Video V1 limited to 21 seconds; V2 (longer clips) not yet shipped

market_signal_strength: High — Midjourney has largest AI image creation user base. Image+video integration at low cost is a significant market shift.
relevance_to_us: High — image+video creative platform integration is directly relevant for our creative tool stack. Pricing model (per-credit video = same as image) is an important reference point.
one_line_judgment: Midjourney V8.1 Alpha ships 5x faster 2K image generation and video creation at 25x lower cost than Runway — consolidating image+video creative production in one tool.

name: xAI Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 + Custom Voice Cloning
launch_signal: April 23: Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0. April 30: Custom Voices + Voice Library (within collection window). Voice APIs (STT+TTS) launched April 17.
core_positioning: Real-time enterprise voice AI stack with custom voice cloning — from short recordings to production-ready voices across 20+ languages
problem_it_solves: Enterprise voice agents require separate vendors for STT, TTS, and voice cloning; creative tools need affordable custom voice generation
user_visibility: Medium — developer API access; Grok Voice Think Fast for enterprise ($300/mo SuperGrok Heavy or API)
access_barrier_change: Custom Voices (Apr 30) allows anyone with short audio recording to clone a voice. API access for STT/TTS. Think Fast 1.0 for enterprise.
workflow_change: Developers integrate xAI voice stack as single vendor for STT+TTS+cloning. Custom voice cloning from short recordings eliminates studio recording requirements.
distribution_change: Same infrastructure powers Grok, Tesla, Starlink — commercial reliability signal. API available via xAI and OpenRouter.
new_product_form: Integrated voice AI platform (STT + TTS + cloning) at aggressive price points
interaction_pattern: Short audio clip → Custom Voice → TTS with expressive tags ([laugh], [sigh], [whisper]); STT with word-level timestamps and speaker diarization
user_scenarios: Podcast creator clones their voice from 30-second recording — generates voiced episodes from text scripts
Game studio creates 5 character voices with custom cloning — no voice actor sessions needed
Creative tool adds voice interface using xAI STT/TTS as single vendor at $0.10/hr STT + $4.20/1M chars TTS
business_model: API: STT $0.10-0.20/hr, TTS $4.20/1M characters. SuperGrok Heavy $300/mo includes Grok Voice Think Fast.
risk_caveat: Voice cloning raises ethical/consent concerns. Error rates from xAI benchmarks (self-reported). Think Fast 1.0 enterprise pricing is high.

good: Custom voice cloning from short recordings seen as major creative tool enabler
Aggressive STT/TTS pricing vs ElevenLabs praised
bad: Voice cloning ethics and consent framework not clearly stated
Self-reported error rate benchmarks need independent validation

market_signal_strength: Medium-high — voice cloning from short recordings at low cost is a meaningful creative tool enabler
relevance_to_us: High — custom voice cloning + STT/TTS API stack directly relevant for audio/voice features in creative tools. Within-window launch (Apr 30 Custom Voices).
one_line_judgment: xAI's Custom Voice Cloning (Apr 30) enables voice generation from short recordings at $4.20/1M chars — a significant cost reduction for creative voice production.

name: Etsy ChatGPT App Integration
launch_signal: May 5, 2026 — Etsy launches native app inside ChatGPT, enabling 100M+ listing search via natural language
core_positioning: Native ChatGPT app that converts natural language creative/product discovery into Etsy marketplace search
problem_it_solves: Traditional keyword search doesn't match how creatives describe what they want; discovery of handmade/creative items requires browsing
user_visibility: High — available to all ChatGPT users (beta). Etsy has 100M+ listings.
access_barrier_change: Beta — available to ChatGPT users without separate Etsy account (browse-only). Full purchasing requires Etsy account.
workflow_change: Natural language: 'find a ceramic mug with an abstract design for under $30' → ChatGPT uses Etsy app to return matching products
distribution_change: Etsy gains ChatGPT distribution channel (200M+ users). Signal of ChatGPT becoming a platform for retail/commerce apps.
new_product_form: ChatGPT native commerce app (not just link-out, but integrated product search inside the chat)
interaction_pattern: Describe product in natural language inside ChatGPT → Etsy app searches 100M+ listings → results appear in chat
user_scenarios: Creative director searches 'handmade stamps for my zine project' in ChatGPT — Etsy results appear without leaving the chat
Designer briefs ChatGPT on a project mood → asks it to find relevant handmade materials from Etsy
business_model: Etsy commission model on transactions driven via ChatGPT channel. ChatGPT platform revenue via OpenAI.
risk_caveat: Beta status. Commerce in chat is early pattern — conversion rate unknown. Excludes most creative professional tools (only consumer marketplace).

good: Natural language product discovery in ChatGPT seen as intuitive for creative users
bad: Beta — limited features, no direct purchase
Etsy's AI push is broader trend, not isolated to ChatGPT

market_signal_strength: Medium — strong signal for ChatGPT-as-platform trend; limited direct creative tool relevance
relevance_to_us: Low-medium — signals ChatGPT becoming a platform for third-party apps (relevant for our potential distribution). Etsy's creative marketplace is tangentially relevant to our creative tool audience.
one_line_judgment: Etsy launches a native ChatGPT app on May 5 — natural language discovery across 100M+ handmade listings signals ChatGPT's platform ambition for creative commerce.

name: Airbyte — Rowboat AI Work App (Startup)
launch_signal: Product Hunt launch April 21, 2026 — 86 upvotes, #26 for the day. Living knowledge graph for meetings, emails, and notes.
core_positioning: AI work app that builds a living knowledge graph from meetings, emails, and notes to handle tasks without re-explanation
problem_it_solves: Users must re-explain context to AI every session; meeting notes, emails, project context scattered across tools
user_visibility: Low — Product Hunt launch, indie product
access_barrier_change: Public launch on Product Hunt. Early access product.
workflow_change: Automatic context capture from meetings/emails/notes → AI that handles drafting, note-taking, project management without re-explaining
new_product_form: Persistent AI work assistant with living knowledge graph
interaction_pattern: Connect meeting notes and email → AI builds context graph → query or delegate tasks in natural language
user_scenarios: Creative director gets meeting notes automatically added to project context — delegates follow-up emails to Rowboat without re-briefing
risk_caveat: Very early stage — indie Product Hunt launch. 86 upvotes is modest signal. Outside collection window (Apr 21).

good: 86 upvotes on Product Hunt shows genuine interest in the problem
bad: Very early stage
Outside collection window

relevance_to_us: Medium — knowledge graph pattern for persistent AI context is relevant to our skill/workflow memory system design.
one_line_judgment: Rowboat is an indie AI work app building a living knowledge graph from meetings and emails — a startup signal for persistent AI context management.

name: Submit.DIY — AI Launch Platform for Indie Makers (Startup)
launch_signal: Product Hunt launch April 17, 2026 — 214 upvotes, #5 for the day. All-in-one AI launch platform for makers.
core_positioning: AI-generated launch copy + tracking across 160+ platforms for indie makers and small teams
problem_it_solves: Indie makers spend weeks manually writing launch copy for each platform (Product Hunt, HN, Twitter, etc.) and lose track of submissions
user_visibility: Medium for indie maker community — #5 on Product Hunt is strong signal
access_barrier_change: Public product. Indie/solo founder price point.
workflow_change: Input product details → AI generates ready-to-publish copy for 160+ channels → track all submissions in dashboard
distribution_change: Targets indie maker distribution strategy; not a big-tech product
new_product_form: AI-powered launch toolkit for indie makers
interaction_pattern: Product description → AI copy generation per platform → submission tracking dashboard
user_scenarios: Solo developer launches AI creative tool — uses Submit.DIY to generate 20 platform-specific descriptions and track all submissions
risk_caveat: Outside collection window (Apr 17). Indie/solo — limited team and resources. Niche product for makers, not mainstream creative tool.

good: 214 upvotes / #5 Product Hunt is strong community validation for a maker tool
bad: Niche use case
Outside collection window

relevance_to_us: Low — tangentially relevant as a signal of AI-enabled indie maker tooling. Included to satisfy startup diversity rule.
one_line_judgment: Submit.DIY is a #5 Product Hunt indie tool that auto-generates AI launch copy for 160+ platforms — strong maker-community signal for AI-powered distribution tools.

name: Image AI Models Drive App Growth (Market Signal)
company: Multiple (Google Gemini, OpenAI ChatGPT, Meta AI)
launch_signal: May 4, 2026 TechCrunch + Appfigures report: image AI releases drive 6.5x more downloads than text model upgrades
core_positioning: Industry data signal: visual AI features are now the primary growth driver for AI mobile apps
problem_it_solves: N/A — this is a market analysis, not a product launch
user_visibility: High awareness — widely covered industry data
distribution_change: Signal that image model releases are the highest-ROI acquisition drivers for AI app growth
user_scenarios: Internal: use image feature launches as growth lever for creative tool user acquisition
risk_caveat: Downloads ≠ revenue: Gemini Nano Banana drove 22M downloads but only $181K in consumer spending. ChatGPT images drove $70M — strong monetization gap between apps.

good: Data confirms image AI as acquisition driver — consistent with viral social media patterns
ChatGPT alone converted downloads to $70M revenue — best case benchmark
bad: Revenue conversion from image AI downloads is inconsistent
Meta AI Vibes drove downloads but no meaningful revenue

market_signal_strength: Very high — Appfigures dataset + TechCrunch coverage within collection window (May 4)
relevance_to_us: High — directly relevant for product growth strategy. Image-led feature launches are the proven growth lever for AI creative tools. Revenue conversion data informs our monetization expectations.
one_line_judgment: Appfigures data (May 4): image AI model releases drive 6.5x more app downloads than text upgrades — confirmed growth playbook for AI creative tool launches.
